---
title: "Hands-on: Workflow Management"
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Workflow Demo: One Pipeline, Two Engines

In this hands-on you will run the same 3-step analysis pipeline using two different workflow engines — **Nextflow** and **Snakemake** — and observe how each one manages execution order, caching, and re-runs.

The pipeline is identical in both cases:

```
example_data.csv
      │
      ▼
 1_derive.py  (Python)  →  1_derived.csv   # mean-centre the values
                                │
                                ▼
                       2_summary.R  (R)  →  2_summary.txt   # summarise the centred values
                                                  │
                                │
                                ▼
                 3_polars_process.py  (Python/polars)  →  3_processed.csv   # add a polars-derived feature
                                                  │
                            ┌─────────────────────┘
                            ▼
                      4_report.qmd  (Quarto)  →  4_report.html   # collect outputs into a report
```

You do **not** need to understand the Python or R code in detail — the focus is on the workflow layer around them.

For the workshop, step 3 intentionally introduces an extra dependency (`polars`) that may not be installed by default.

---

## Nextflow

**Directory:** `demo/nextflow/`

The pipeline is defined in `main.nf`. Each step is a `process` block with explicit `input:` and `output:` declarations. Nextflow runs every process in an isolated subdirectory under `work/` and copies final outputs to `results/` via `publishDir`.

### Codespaces

If you want to run this in **GitHub Codespaces**, create a Codespace and select the Dev Container configuration:

- `Operra Module 3 - Nextflow demo` (from `.devcontainer/nextflow/devcontainer.json`)

### Running

```bash
# Build the image and start the container (first run only)
docker compose up -d

# Open a shell inside the container
docker compose exec nextflow bash

# Run the pipeline
nextflow run main.nf
```

Results are written to `results/4_report.html`.

### Re-running after a change

Nextflow caches every task by content hash in `work/`. Pass `-resume` to skip unchanged steps:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf -resume
```

If you edit `2_summary.R`, only `R_SUMMARY` and `FINAL_REPORT` re-run — `PY_DERIVE` is served from cache.

---

## Snakemake

**Directory:** `demo/snakemake/`

The pipeline is defined in `Snakefile`. Each step is a `rule` block. Snakemake infers execution order by matching `output:` of one rule to `input:` of the next. Intermediate outputs go to `work/`, the final report to `results/`.

### Codespaces

If you want to run this in **GitHub Codespaces**, create a Codespace and select the Dev Container configuration:

- `Operra Module 3 - Snakemake demo` (from `.devcontainer/snakemake/devcontainer.json`)

### Running

```bash
# Build the image and start the container (first run only)
docker compose up -d

# Open a shell inside the container
docker compose exec snakemake bash

# Run the pipeline
snakemake --cores 1
```

If you run Snakemake from outside `demo/snakemake/`, pass an explicit directory and Snakefile:

```bash
snakemake \
  --directory /workspace/contents/module_3_workflow_management/demo/snakemake \
  --snakefile /workspace/contents/module_3_workflow_management/demo/snakemake/Snakefile \
  --cores 1
```

Results are written to `results/4_report.html`.

### Re-running after a change

Snakemake tracks file modification timestamps. Just re-run — it automatically skips steps whose outputs are newer than their inputs:

```bash
snakemake --cores 1
```

To force a specific rule to re-run regardless of timestamps:

```bash
snakemake --cores 1 --forcerun r_summary
```

---

## Key differences

| | Nextflow | Snakemake |
|---|---|---|
| Pipeline file | `main.nf` | `Snakefile` |
| Step isolation | Each task runs in its own `work/<hash>/` subdirectory | Steps run in the project directory |
| Intermediate outputs | Staged in `work/`, copied to `results/` via `publishDir` | Written directly to `work/` |
| Cache mechanism | Content hashes (task inputs + code) | File modification timestamps |
| Re-run only changed steps | `nextflow run main.nf -resume` | `snakemake --cores 1` (automatic) |
| Force re-run one step | Not directly — delete its `work/` entry or use `-resume` after touching the script | `--forcerun <rule_name>` |
